englishRequirements.waiverNotes: International undergraduate applicants whose first language is not English are evaluated for English proficiency during review. Purdue also considers demonstrated academic success in an English-speaking curriculum, and transfer credit may be used as an alternative proof path in some cases.

scholarshipNotes: Purdue awards university-wide merit scholarships and also offers need-based aid and departmental scholarships. In general, international undergraduate students are not eligible for financial aid, including scholarships.

testingRequirements.latestSatTestDateNote: If able, take SAT in the spring of junior year in preparation for the Nov. 1 Early Action deadline.
testingRequirements.latestActTestDateNote: If able, take ACT in the spring of junior year in preparation for the Nov. 1 Early Action deadline.

identity.officialAdmissionsUrl.excerpt: Freshman Admission Criteria - Undergraduate Admissions - Purdue University

applicationRounds.excerpt: First-Time College Student (Freshmen) Application ... Early Action Application Deadline ... Regular Decision Application Deadline

deadlinesByRound.excerpt: November 1: Early Action Application Deadline ... January 15: Regular Decision Application Deadline

englishRequirements.minimumIelts.excerpt: Generally, applicants have a score of 6.5 or higher with a minimum score of 6.0 in each section.

englishRequirements.minimumToeflInternet.excerpt: For exams taken before January 21, 2026: We require a minimum TOEFL iBT score of 88 or higher with at least a 20 or higher per section.

englishRequirements.waiverNotes.excerpt: International undergraduate applicants whose first language is not English will be evaluated for English language proficiency during the application review process.

testPolicy.excerpt: Purdue expects applicants to have SAT or ACT scores.

requiredMaterials.excerpt: A complete application includes the application itself, Purdue-specific questions, self-reported high school grades, self-reported ACT or SAT test scores, the application fee or qualified fee waiver, and an essay.

tuitionAnnualUsd.excerpt: Purdue West Lafayette first-year undergraduates: Tuition: Nonresident $31,830

estimatedCostOfAttendanceUsd.excerpt: Purdue West Lafayette first-year undergraduates: Tuition $31,830 + Required Fees $274 + Food and housing (on-campus) $16,734 + Books and supplies $1,090 + Transportation $570 + Other expenses $2,200 = $52,698

scholarshipAvailabilityFlag.excerpt: Purdue awards merit scholarships and also considers applicants for scholarships with need-based components.

scholarshipNotes.excerpt: First-year merit scholarships are awarded based on a holistic review ... Purdue scholarships are highly competitive ... in general, international undergraduate students are not eligible for financial aid, including scholarships.

recommendationInputs.internationalAidPol.excerpt: In general, international undergraduate students are not eligible for financial aid, including scholarships.

recommendationInputs.hasNeedBasedAid.excerpt: Purdue's Division of Financial Aid administers need-based aid from federal, state and Purdue funding sources.

recommendationInputs.programFitTags.excerpt: Purdue Engineering is recognized for innovation, research and high-impact engineering education.
Purdue's Mitch Daniels School of Business prepares students for leadership and innovation in business.
Purdue is a leading research university driving discovery across disciplines.

recommendationInputs.programAdmissionMod.excerpt: Some majors are competitive and may have additional requirements or limited space.

recommendationInputs.testingRequirements.excerpt: We accept SAT or ACT scores and have no preference on which test is taken.
If able, you should take one or the other in the spring of your junior year in preparation for the November 1 Early Action deadline.
If able, you should take one or the other in the spring of your junior year in preparation for the November 1 Early Action deadline.
You may report the best scores from across different tests.
This application also requires you to provide an essay and answer Purdue-specific questions.
A complete application includes self-reported ACT or SAT test scores.
